title: salix aquatica var. ovata
intro: Salix aquatica var. ovata, also known as the Riverbank Willow or Ovate River Willow, is a variety of the Salix aquatica plant, a type of willow tree that grows in wet environments along rivers and streams. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. The health benefits of Salix aquatica var. ovata include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant protection. Salix aquatica var. ovata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the bark being used to make a decoction and the leaves being used to make a tea, making it a versatile and valuable herb in natural medicine.
